candidates.stmt:
Every stage of my life has been impacted by London. I am a Londoner by birth, being born at Whipps Cross Hospital in Walthamstow. I studied my undergraduate degree in History at Queen Mary, University of London. I currently live with my partner in Stoke Newington.

I have worked in Parliament as part of an MP's office and have seen how political representatives can affect positive change and results for residents and our communities.

I work as a community engagement specialist for projects in both the private and public sectors. My focus is to ensure that credible engagement with any communities are undertaken - something that has been lacking from our Labour-run councils in Hackney, Islington and Waltham Forest.

For too long policy makers have ignored the wishes of their residents, disregarding the results of public consultations and denying democracy.

As your representative on the GLA for North East London, I would have residents hold me to my pledges and ensure that they were properly consulted on any policies impacting their lives and wellbeing, that the results were abided by and that promised outcomes were always delivered.

George has lived in Hackney since childhood, brought up by queer parents and enjoying the incredible parks, vibrant play spaces, strong sense of community, and brilliant local venues and businesses in Clissold Ward. George works in Legal Aid representing vulnerable people seeking justice, including asylum seekers, victims of trafficking, and those in prison, holding power to account. He would bring that experience as a caseworker to serve constituents, while fighting to make the borough greener, more just, and more democratic.

George says, “I believe in Green values of hope, stewardship and solidarity, and I would work to ensure every resident is heard, without fear or favour.”

I am a Reform UK councillor candidate for Hackney, standing with conviction, clarity, and a strong commitment to restoring accountability, pride, and common-sense leadership in local government.
With over a decade of membership in the Conservative Party—where I served as an active campaigner and political organiser—I made a principled decision to join Reform UK because I believe it represents a more honest, grounded, and determined approach to putting the public first. I have not followed trends or convenience; I have followed principle.

I am a firm believer in traditional values, family, responsibility, and civic duty. I stand for leadership that is rooted in structure, respect for community, and the belief that local people should always come first in local decisions.

My professional background spans senior and leadership roles across media and property, including experience within high-performance environments in broadcasting and real estate. I have worked in and around major organisations in both the media and property sectors, where discipline, results, and accountability are non-negotiable.

I am a campaigner, activist, and leader—not a career politician. I understand what it takes to lead teams, deliver outcomes, and operate under pressure. I bring that same mindset into public service.

My mission is clear: to restore pride in Hackney, strengthen local accountability, and ensure that residents feel represented, respected, and prioritised. I am here to serve the community with seriousness, discipline, and unwavering commitment to real change.

I have lived in the area all my life and went to the Benthal Primary School. I work for an international animal welfare charity. I am Vice-Chair of the Hackney Downs Park Users Group. I have been a regular volunteer at the Hackney Winter Night Shelter. I have run youth media workshops in schools, pupil referral units and community groups both in and outside the borough. 

Only by voting Green in Hackney Downs ward can you ensure you have a hard-working, independent voice that will keep the council accountable. A one party-state council means Labour will be marking their own homework and not delivering value for money for residents.

Dylan grew up in Hackney, living in council housing and raised by a single mother. His view of politics reflects the passion and cynicism of many young Londoners. He has experienced everyday challenges like insufficient housing and an education system that fails to deliver equal opportunities to everyone. Dylan was surprised to learn he could walk into Town Hall and watch a committee meeting. When he did, he was warmly greeted by a Green Councillor; his political engagement blossomed from there.

Dylan says, “I want people like me to see they can lead. I want young people to have someone in Town Hall who they can come talk to.”

The failure of the Tory manifesto to provide a clear unifying statement to bring people together contrasts with Labour's radical agenda to unite the nation. We will:

* Abolish tuition fees * Retain the 'triple lock' on pensions and winter fuel allowance * Improve pay and conditions for nurses and other public sector workers  * Re-nationalise key utilities to save the public from continuing exploitation

It is astonishing that Theresa May linked security against terrorism, etc in her naive Article 50 letter, which understandably put the backs up of even sympathetic EU countries. She has also failed to confirm immediate retention of rights for EU citizens here - working in hospitals, hospitality, on building sites, etc. Instead, she has raised the issue of foxhunting, which I strongly oppose and deplore, being debated again in Parliament. 

Many Tory MP's claimed to support vulnerable refugees, but 289 voted against the Dubs amendment agreeing to help 3,000 children and young people fleeing persecution, settle here. Among them, was Faversham and Mid Kent's MP.

Labour is gathering support, can bring people together - and make a final decision on Brexit after our brilliant shadow cabinet member, Sir Keir Starmer, concludes negotiations.

I made a song about the Brexit shambles with EU musicians, its on You Tube: "Little Englanders". Let's make sure the Tories, who are taking the electorate for granted, are singing from a different songsheet on June 9th!

Tyrone Scott is Head of Campaigns at War on Want, acting on global inequality, climate injustice and justice for Palestinians. He previously worked at Shelter, and alongside his work with Hackney Greens has fought for housing justice, defended tenants’ rights, and challenged bad landlords.

Tyrone served on the Young Greens Executive Committee, and chaired the Global Majority Greens, shaping Green Party policy. He stood as the Green parliamentary candidate for Hackney South and Shoreditch in 2019, and is respected in the party and beyond as a campaigner and community organiser. Tyrone also stood to be Deputy Leader of the Green Party in 2022, placing second behind Zack Polanski.

He says, “Hackney Wick deserves bold representation— standing up for tenants, protecting our community spaces, and building a greener, fairer borough for everyone.”

_Hackney Wick is a vibrant, diverse community built on **creativity**, **resilience** and **ambition**, from our estates to our waterways._ _As your Labour and Co-operative councillors, we are re-standing in May 2026 to continue delivering for you._

_We are **visible**, **accessible** and **accountable**._ _We hold regular surgeries, knock on doors and support local groups to take action on the issues that matter most._ _At a time when many feel left behind, we are standing up for safer streets, more social housing, a cleaner and more sustainable Wick, and a stronger community._

_We will always put residents first, making sure regeneration works for the many, not just a few._ _We are not just asking for your vote._ _We are asking to keep working alongside you to make Hackney Wick safer, cleaner and fairer for everyone._

_Hackney Wick is a vibrant, diverse community built on **creativity**, **resilience** and **ambition**, from our estates to our waterways._ _As your Labour and Co-operative councillors, we are re-standing in May 2026 to continue delivering for you._

_We are **visible**, **accessible** and **accountable**._ _We hold regular surgeries, knock on doors and support local groups to take action on the issues that matter most._ _At a time when many feel left behind, we are standing up for safer streets, more social housing, a cleaner and more sustainable Wick, and a stronger community._

_We will always put residents first, making sure regeneration works for the many, not just a few._ _We are not just asking for your vote._ _We are asking to keep working alongside you to make Hackney Wick safer, cleaner and fairer for everyone._

_We are **visible**, **accessible** and **accountable**._ _We hold regular surgeries, knock on doors and support local groups to take action on the issues that matter most._ _At a time when many feel left behind, we are standing up for safer streets, more social housing, a cleaner and more sustainable Wick, and a stronger community._

_We will always put residents first, making sure regeneration works for the many, not just a few._ _We are not just asking for your vote._ _We are asking to keep working alongside you to make Hackney Wick safer, cleaner and fairer for everyone._

Midnight has lived in Haggerston for over 5 years and has immersed herself into the Hackney community. She has been a committed volunteer with Hackney Citizens Advice and her local foodbank and takes pride in her role as a trust governor of Hackney New Primary School. With a legal background and her current work in human rights law, Midnight is interested in social justice and anti-discrimination and is also an advocate for mental wellbeing.

Midnight is passionate about housing. She was brought up on council estates and is now a private renter with a strong belief that a comfortable, safe, secure home for all is a fundamental right. Midnight has listened to the concerns of Haggerston residents on the doorstep and looks forward to representing your voice at Hackney council. As a nature enthusiast, Midnight values Hackney’s green spaces and aims to work to preserve these and enhance the environment.

Midnight is looking forward to supporting Haggerston’s diverse residents and making a real difference to people’s lives. Midnight is keen to work with residents, families and community groups to make Hackney a great place to live and a borough to be proud of.

Zoe has spent her working life as a journalist and researcher advocating for people to be heard. With a PhD on British intervention in the Arab world, she has published widely on international political issues, worked as a translator of Arabic to English and campaigned on issues of borders, Palestine and human rights locally and abroad. She grew up in Melbourne, but Hackney has been her home for over ten years.

She says, “I’m excited about practising a new, hopeful form of politics in Hackney and making sure our council listens. As a councillor for Homerton, I will be an advocate for greater housing equity, health and well-being, for sustainability and ethical investment.”

Heather was born in the Mother’s Hospital in Hackney and has lived on the Pembury Estate since 2008. She works as a freelance education researcher, teacher and administrator. She is an active local campaigner, being a founding member of Morning Lane People’s Space which is working to get our local Hackney Community voice heard in the proposals for the development of the 55 Morning Lane Tesco site. She was also one of the organisers of the Hackney Divest Camp for Palestine that occupied Hackney Town Hall Square for two months of 2024. She is active in her Residents Association. She is standing with the support of Hackney Green Party as a Hackney Independent Socialist member - a new political party that focuses on improving the lives of people in the borough.

Mission Hackney - priority is you!

***LTNs must go ***
LTNs clear, realistic policies: https://vahidalmasihackney.org/policies

***Safer Hackney***
https://vahidalmasihackney.org/safer-hackney

I’ve spent the last decade in leadership roles within worldwide organisations, shaping successful strategies, creating opportunities, driving growth, and pushing teams to build and deliver real results.

Now I’m ready to bring that experience to our Hackney Council.

As your Mayor, my priority will be simple:

It’s YOU!

To protect your money, bring order to the council’s finances, improve local services and do everything in my power to reduce crime across Hackney. Hackney is carrying over £300 million in debt, with around £200,000 a week being spent on interest alone. That is money that should be helping residents,

building more homes, improving streets, and investing in our future. 
Every one of us deserves to feel safe in our street, safe in our home, and safe in our community. I will push for tougher action on crime and a council that works properly with police to make our borough safer.

Hackney has had enough of excuses. We want to see less crime, improve services, and make this borough the trendiest, best-run neighbourhood in London.

Bettina Maidment has lived in Hackney for ten years with her family and is the founder of Plastic Free Hackney. 

VOTE GREEN TO HOLD THE LABOUR COUNCIL TO ACCOUNT

At the last election a swing of just 46 votes would have elected your first Green councillor. This time we want you to elect all three of us to give you a strong Green team for Hackney Downs. 

We work hard all year round to help residents on everything from housing issues to crime. 

Labour will continue to run the council after this election. Hackney Downs is one of few wards that can elect anything except another Labour councillor. If you elect Greens, we will hold the council to account and make your voice heard.
Sally Zlotowitz has lived in Hackney for 15 years. She works in mental health with young people, and particularly on countering violence.

Hi there,

I'm Rafie Faruq - as an Independent, I'm a different kind of councillor. Rather than taking sides, I listen to problems from local people on a case by case basis.

As a yoga and meditation teacher, I've learned to really understand with empathy the heart of issues and people's concerns. I've also run over 100 community song circle events, bringing together strangers and creating community, particularly in times of loneliness.

On the other hand, I've also been running the tech for good business Genie, for nearly 9 years. To date we've helped over 200,000 small businesses get cheaper access to legal services.

In Hackney, I've heard people care about striking the balance between improving safety and gentrification, versus not overunning the venues and markets we know and love, that represent the culture, art and heart of Hackney.

There are also over 3000 familes living in temporary accommodation and nearly half of all children on free school meals, a much higher poverty rate than the rest of London.

These issues, and many more are ones I hope to take a close look at as your councillor. For more information visit rafiefaruq.com - and let's keep the heart in hackney!

Rolf Dekker (he/him) is raising his young children in Victoria, Hackney. A digital marketer originally from the Netherlands, his background in the Dutch Green Party (GroenLinks) fostered his pragmatic approach to community problems. A committed daily cyclist and parent, he is an active member of the 'Safer Roads and Clean Air in South Hackney' campaign. Rolf believes in listening to locals, especially when LTN schemes risk dividing communities.

Rolf says: “Listening and mediation is how we fix Hackney’s traffic, making it safer and more accessible for residents and businesses. Casework is about solving everyday problems—from schools in need to unsafe junctions—with total transparency. I aim to bridge the gap between Green policy and the lived realities of Hackney residents.”

Penny Wrout lives in Victoria Ward, which she has represented for nearly 8 years as a councillor in Hackney, working to help residents with housing, transport and waste problems. She left what she saw as a tired and complacent Labour group on Hackney Council 2 years ago and formed the Hackney Independent Socialists, who've worked closely with Hackney Green group to be an effective opposition on the Council.  She works hard to help residents with housing, transport, waste and care problems and says, 'We’re living in an age when people feel increasingly disempowered so I’d like to continue as a councillor and help people get what’s rightfully theirs.  If we win the Council alongside the Greens and a Green Mayor, we’d have a real chance with a fresh administration to breathe new life into Hackney services like housing and social care and make a tangible difference to thousands of people’s lives."

DR THANGAVELU SENTHIL KUMAR - “The Strongest Voice to Deliver Real Change”
Independent Candidate for Woodberry Down Ward Councilor Election 
on 7 May 2026: 7am to 10pm

Who Am I?
I am a frontline NHS doctor with lived experience of serving the communities in the UK for the past 25 years. I have worked across the UK in various hospitals often at the deepest end, helping people during difficult and challenging times in their lives.

I believe politics should be about service, honesty, and accountability —. I chose to stand as an Independent who is directly accountable, easily accessible and answers to Woodberry Down residents — not to party bosses, ego, hierarchy or propaganda.

Independent does NOT mean powerless. Independent means accountable directly to YOU. If elected I will have the freedom to speak honestly without permission from any party higher-ups, fearlessly challenge injustice, be fully committed to be your voice when you need someone to stand up and speak for you and focus entirely for your needs.

Why I Am Contesting This Election?
I have experienced months of homelessness right here in this Woodberry down ward. I have felt invisible, overlooked, and systemically and systematically let down by the very systems that were supposed to help. I know what that feels like. And I never want you to feel it alone. Now I want to serve Woodberry Down as your councillor — not as a party member following orders, but as your neighbour, your advocate, and your voice.

I believe our Woodberry Down community deserves better access, stronger representation, and practical solutions rather than empty promises repeated over decades. “Doing the same thing over and over again and expecting different results is insanity.” – Albert Einstein. Remember if you have the same people doing the same things, you should expect the same results. It is now time for that long elusive CHANGE.

I previously served within a political party, driven by a sincere desire to help people and improve
